text: What strategies did you use to solve this problem? How do they compare to strategies you used in previous problems?

Continue building expertise in working with unit rates to solve ratio problems of greater complexity.

Work to find needed equivalent ratios using a variety of strategies, including

  • unit rates;
  • ratio tables;
  • multiplying/dividing both quantities in a ratio (scaling) to find larger or smaller values as needed;
  • expression or equations (not asked for in Problem 3.4); and
  • other informal strategies based on multiplicative reasoning and number sense needed to compose and decompose given values to find needed values.

Did You Know?

In July 2022, in Alexandria, Virginia the first backward-running race was held in the United States. The runners ran on an oval track at Edison High School in Alexandria.

The runners races in the mile, 100, 200, 400, and 800-meter events. At the starting pistol, they all ran facing astern, many not looking back but instead using the track lines to guide them.

Bob Draim, one of the organizers of the race, claims the sport is kinder to the body. Draim and Aaron Yoder who hold the 5:30 world record started planning for the race in Alexandria in 2021. The sport is gaining popularity in this country. Yoder claims that "Instead of looking at how far you need to go, sometimes it's important to see how far you've come in life," he said.
Source: Gupta, G., "For These Runners, the Only Way Forward Is Backward." Washington Post. July 9, 2022
